---
title: "Simon Hamra"
subtitle: "Applied Business Analytics · Machine Learning"
format:
  typst:
    fontsize: 10pt
    margin:
      x: 1.7cm
      y: 1.6cm
    mainfont: "DejaVu Sans"
---

```{=typst}
#show heading: set text(fill: rgb("#4F46E5"))
#show heading.where(level: 2): set text(size: 11pt)
#set par(leading: 0.62em)
#v(-6pt)
```

Boston, MA · simhamra\@gmail.com · github.com/simonhamra · linkedin.com/in/simonhamra

## Summary

Business analytics master student with a background in business and marketing. I work with large labor market data in PySpark and I am moving into machine learning. I have a solid base in statistics and I try to explain results in clear, simple terms. I am looking for a first role in machine learning or data.

## Skills

- **Programming:** Python (intermediate: pandas, scikit-learn, TensorFlow / Keras), R, SQL
- **Machine learning:** regression, tree models, neural networks, transformers (learning)
- **Big data:** PySpark, Spark SQL
- **Cloud and tools:** AWS EC2, Power BI, Quarto, Git
- **Foundations:** statistics and probability

## Education

- **MS, Applied Business Analytics** — Boston University, Metropolitan College *(current)*
- **MSc, Marketing** — HULT International Business School
- **BSc, Management** — Rennes School of Business

## Experience

- **Social media and content — OneEvent, London.** Managed the public image of DJs on social media. Created reels and posts and helped grow their reach.
- **E-commerce and marketing intern — Fereira, France.** SEO and online marketing for an e-commerce shop.

## Selected projects (Boston University)

- **Salary insights at scale.** Cleaned a 700 MB Lightcast file in PySpark and charted pay across 72,498 job postings.
- **Predicting salary.** Built linear, polynomial and random forest models, and found and fixed a multicollinearity problem that blocked the model statistics.
- **A job market in SQL.** Built a star schema from one flat file and answered four labor market questions in Spark SQL.

## Languages

French (native) · English (professional)
